# Research - 2026-06-13

Focus: **all** (CLI, providers, tools, MCP, commands, hooks, skills, plugins, agents, permissions)

> 2-day delta on 2026-06-11. Auto-implement queue is **empty**
> (`gh issue list --label auto-implement --state open` -> `[]`).
> 4 mechanically transferable upstream items in 48h: cline #11480
> head+tail tool-output cap, cline #11463 strip command echo from
> `run_commands`, opencode #32074 prefer MCP `structuredContent`,
> opencode #30676 add `cwd` to local MCP servers. claude-code: 0
> functional commits (CHANGELOG bot only); aider: model
> registration only.

## Executive Summary

- **cline #11480 -> Item #1 (~4h)**: `truncateOutput`
  (`src/tool/index.ts:102`) keeps **first** N lines; failures live
  at the END. Switch to head+tail.
- **cline #11463 -> Item #2 (~2h)**: `bash.ts:188-225` and
  `shell.ts:194-231` echo input `command` back inside `output`.
  Heredoc file-generation duplicates source in every cached turn.
- **opencode #32074 -> Item #3 (~3h)**: `client.ts:434` ignores
  `result.structuredContent`. Servers returning only structured
  content appear empty.
- **opencode #30676 -> Item #4 (~2h)**: `McpServerConfig` has no
  `cwd` field; `spawn` inherits `process.cwd()`. Add optional
  `cwd`, resolve relative against `options?.workdir`.
- **Backlog stall is 7 cycles deep** (06-06 -> 06-13). 06-11 brief
  flagged 5-cycle stall; queue is now 0. Worth a `daily-merge-prs`
  warning when `auto-implement` drops to 0 with engineering-agent
  still scheduled.
- **No-action**: claude-code (CHANGELOG bot), aider (Anthropic 4.6
  /4.7 model IDs - we use SAP AI Core IDs). cline #11514 is a
  prompt nudge - defer to Agent 2 audit.

## Detailed Findings

#### 1. Head+tail truncation for tool output (Category: tool, ~4h)

- **What**: Refactor `src/tool/index.ts:102 truncateOutput` from
  `lines.slice(0, MAX_LINES)` to head+tail: keep first
  `MAX_LINES/2` and last `MAX_LINES/2`, elide middle with notice
  reporting original line + byte counts. Same change to
  byte-budget branch. Update truncation hints in `bash.ts:217`,
  `shell.ts:224`, `read.ts:238` to mention elision.
- **Why**: Test/build summaries print at END. Today model sees
  first 2000 lines, misses the failure, retries blindly. Cline
  measured ~30% drop in failed-build re-attempts.
- **Source**: cline/cline#11480 (commit `d20e5178`, 2026-06-12).
- **Category**: tool. **Size**: small (~4h).

#### 2. Strip command echo from bash/shell tool result (Category: tool, ~2h)

- **What**: In `src/tool/tools/bash.ts:188-225` and
  `shell.ts:194-231`, drop `command` from the returned `output`
  object (or replace with `commandLength: <N>`). Provider already
  has the command as tool-call **input**; echoing it back doubles
  bytes in every cached turn. Test: assert
  `result.output.command` absent for inputs > 4 KB.
- **Why**: Heredoc prompts (`cat > x <<'EOF' ...`) duplicate the
  entire source. Over 10 turns this multiplies token spend.
- **Source**: cline/cline#11463 (commit `7f9d5461`, 2026-06-11,
  `+134 -6`).
- **Category**: tool. **Size**: small (~2h).

#### 3. Prefer MCP `structuredContent` over `content` (Category: mcp, ~3h)

- **What**: In `src/mcp/client.ts:433-447`, before flattening
  `result.content`, check `result.structuredContent`. If non-null,
  return `JSON.stringify(structuredContent)` as result text. Fall
  back to current path otherwise. Preserve `result.isError`. Tests
  at `src/mcp/__tests__/structured-content.test.ts`: structured
  only, content only, both (structured wins), null structured
  falls back.
- **Why**: Spec-compliant MCP servers (Qt Creator and several
  Codex-pattern servers) return ONLY `structuredContent`. Today
  `callTool` returns empty string and the model thinks the call
  failed silently.
- **Source**: sst/opencode#32074 (commit `be227503`, 2026-06-12,
  `+9 -3`).
- **Category**: mcp. **Size**: small (~3h).

#### 4. Optional `cwd` on local MCP servers (Category: mcp, ~2h)

- **What**: Add optional `cwd?: string` to `McpServerConfig`
  (`src/mcp/config.ts:14-25`). In `connectStdio`
  (`client.ts:161-186`): resolve relative `cwd` against
  `options?.workdir ?? process.cwd()` via `path.resolve`; absolute
  passes; undefined keeps current behaviour. Pass to both
  `spawn(...)` and `new StdioClientTransport({ cwd, ... })`.
- **Why**: Monorepo subproject MCP servers today need absolute
  paths in `args` because `spawn` inherits `process.cwd()`. Aligns
  with opencode and Claude Desktop config shape.
- **Source**: sst/opencode#30676 (commit `7e7ad377`, 2026-06-11,
  `+46 -11`).
- **Category**: mcp. **Size**: small (~2h).

## Actionable Recommendations

1. **Auto-implement queue=0** for the first time since 06-06. Have
   `daily-merge-prs` warn when open `auto-implement` drops to 0
   while engineering-agent is scheduled - silent "no work" hides
   silent breakage.
2. **Smallest-PR ladder**: #2 (~2h, ~30 LOC) -> #4 (~2h, ~50 LOC)
   -> #3 (~3h, ~60 LOC) -> #1 (~4h, ~120 LOC).
3. **#1 and #2 compose**: head+tail is more useful once commands
   aren't echoing their stdin. Land #2 first or in the same PR.
4. **Reference-repo signal**: aider and claude-code each produced
   0 actionable items in 48h. If next cycle is dry again, demote
   both to weekly polling.
